service_categories.name: All Services

faq_articles.title: Service Areas and ZIP Codes We Cover
faq_articles.content:
We currently provide landscaping, lawn care, and seasonal cleanup services across several metro areas. Our standard service radius is approximately 30–40 minutes from our local branches.

Primary service regions
- Chicago, IL and nearby neighborhoods
- Springfield, IL and surrounding suburbs
- Los Angeles (Westside and nearby), CA
- San Francisco, CA city neighborhoods

Supported ZIP codes (partial list)
Chicago area (IL)
- 60610 (Old Town / Near North)
- 60611, 60614, 60622, 60657

Springfield area (IL)
- 12345 and adjacent ZIPs within a 10–15 mile radius

Los Angeles area (CA)
- 90210 (Beverly Hills)
- 90024, 90025, 90064 and nearby ZIPs along the Westside corridor

San Francisco area (CA)
- 94110 (Mission District and surrounding blocks)
- 94114, 94117, 94131 and adjacent ZIPs

If you do not see your ZIP code listed above, we may still be able to serve you depending on the exact location and scope of work. The quickest way to confirm availability is to:
1. Use the Contact page to send us your full address and ZIP code.
2. Or start a booking or quote request; our system will automatically validate whether your address is inside our service area.

All standard policies, including scheduling rules and cancellations, apply equally across our covered service ZIP codes, including 60610, 12345, 90210, and 94110.

faq_articles.title: Cancellation Policy for Scheduled Services
faq_articles.content:
We understand that plans can change. Our goal is to keep scheduling flexible while ensuring our crews have reliable routes.

Standard cancellation window
- You may cancel or reschedule most services up to 24 hours before the scheduled start time with no cancellation fee.
- Changes made more than 48 hours in advance are always free.

Same day and late cancellations
- If you cancel within 24 hours of the appointment start time, a late cancellation fee of 25 percent of the scheduled service price may apply.
- If our crew arrives on site and is unable to perform the work due to access issues, locked gates, pets, or unsafe conditions, a trip fee (typically 35–50 dollars) may be charged.

Recurring maintenance plans
- For monthly residential or commercial maintenance plans, you can pause or cancel at the end of any current billing cycle with at least 7 days notice before the next renewal.
- Individual visits that are skipped or cancelled with at least 24 hours notice can usually be rescheduled within the same month, subject to route availability.

Weather-related changes
- If severe weather makes it unsafe or impractical to perform the work, we will contact you to reschedule at no additional cost.

How this applies to your area
- The same cancellation rules apply across all of our service regions and ZIP codes, including Chicago ZIP 60610, Springfield ZIP 12345, Los Angeles ZIP 90210, and San Francisco ZIP 94110.

To cancel or reschedule
- Use the link in your confirmation email, or
- Call our office during business hours, or
- Submit a message through the Contact page with your name, service type, date, and preferred new date.

If you have any questions about how this policy applies to a specific booking, please contact us before your scheduled visit.

faq_articles.title: Online Booking, Arrival Windows, and Payment Options
faq_articles.content:
Our online booking tool allows you to reserve one-time services such as spring yard cleanups, leaf removal, and design consultations.

What can be booked online
- Spring Yard Cleanup (One-Time)
- Leaf Removal - One-Time
- Landscape Design Consultation
- General Landscaping Service (Hourly Crew)

Arrival windows
- For most field services, you select a start time, and we provide a 60–120 minute arrival window based on routing.
- Crews aim to arrive as close to the scheduled time as possible; if there is a significant delay, we will notify you by text or phone.

Payment options
- Pay on site: Card or check at the time of service completion, available for most one-time services.
- Pay after service: Secure online payment link sent after the work is completed and approved.
- Prepay by card: For some services, prepayment may be required to hold the booking.

Booking confirmations and reminders
- You will receive an email confirmation with the date, time window, service address, and summary of work.
- Reminder emails or texts are sent 24 hours before your appointment when enabled.

Changes to bookings
- You may update contact details, access instructions, or gate codes via the confirmation link or by contacting our office.

For full details on cancellations and rescheduling, please see the Cancellation Policy article.

faq_articles.title: How Our Pricing and Estimates Work
faq_articles.content:
We use transparent, menu-style pricing for most standard services, and provide custom quotes for larger projects.

Standard services
- Lawn mowing and basic maintenance are priced per month or per visit based on yard size.
- Seasonal cleanups and leaf removal are priced per visit and may include haul-away fees.

Installations and design-build projects
- Front yard and backyard installations, such as drought-tolerant conversions or patios with fire pits, are typically priced as flat project estimates with a range shown on the service detail page.
- Final pricing may vary based on access, slope, material choices, and exact square footage.

Online estimates vs final quotes
- The prices you see online are typical for standard conditions in our core service areas.
- After you request a quote or consultation, a specialist may adjust the estimate once they review photos or visit the site.

Taxes and fees
- Sales tax is applied where required by local regulations.
- Trip or disposal fees, when applicable, will be clearly listed on your estimate or invoice.

Promotions and discounts
- Promo codes such as first-time customer discounts are applied at checkout or on your invoice.
- Only one promotion may typically be applied per service or plan.

If you have a unique property or are unsure which service level fits your budget, we recommend requesting a consultation or contacting us with your ZIP code and a brief description of your yard.

faq_articles.title: Getting Started With Our Landscaping Services
faq_articles.content:
New to our company and not sure where to begin? This overview will help you choose the right next step.

Step 1: Check service availability
- Visit the FAQ article on Service Areas to confirm that we serve your ZIP code.

Step 2: Decide what kind of help you need
- Ongoing lawn mowing and maintenance: Visit the Maintenance Plans page.
- One-time cleanup or seasonal work: Use the Book Now page and choose a cleanup service.
- New landscape installation or patio project: Explore the Gallery for inspiration, then request a consultation.

Step 3: Share a few details
- Providing your address, ZIP code, and preferred time of day helps us schedule efficiently.
- You can also upload photos during the quote or consultation request process when available.

Step 4: Review your estimate
- For many services, pricing is shown upfront; more complex projects will receive a custom estimate after a site review.

If you are ever unsure which service is the best fit, contact us and we will recommend options tailored to your property and budget.

faq_articles.title: Accounts, Billing, and Communication Preferences
faq_articles.content:
You are not required to create an account to book a one-time service, but having an account makes it easier to manage recurring plans and view past work.

Accounts
- With an account, you can see upcoming visits, past invoices, and saved project ideas from the Gallery.

Billing
- We accept major credit cards, checks, and certain digital wallets in select regions.
- Recurring maintenance plans are billed automatically according to your selected frequency (monthly, quarterly, or annually where available).

Communication preferences
- You can choose to receive reminders and updates by email, SMS, or phone call.
- Your preferred contact method can be set during checkout or updated later by contacting our support team.

If you need help updating billing information or communication preferences, please reach out via the Contact page.

service_areas.notes: Primary Chicago service zone covering Old Town / Near North; full range of mowing, cleanups, and installations available.
Downtown / Streeterville area; services focus on townhomes and small shared courtyards.
Lincoln Park neighborhood; full residential maintenance and project services.
Wicker Park / Ukrainian Village area; small and medium residential yards.
Lakeview neighborhood; popular area for recurring lawn maintenance plans.
Chicago Loop high-rise district; limited services for select rooftop and courtyard projects only.
Core Springfield service ZIP; full catalog of mowing, cleanups, and installations available.
Primary Westside Los Angeles service area; frequent drought-tolerant front yard installations.
Westwood area; mix of residential maintenance and design-build projects.
Sawtelle / West LA; standard lawn care and seasonal cleanups available.
Rancho Park / Cheviot Hills area; ideal for medium-size yard projects and patio installs.
Mission District; focus on compact front and backyard spaces, including drought-tolerant designs.
Castro / Noe Valley; sloped yards and terraced gardens are common project types here.
Haight-Ashbury / Cole Valley; smaller lots with custom design-build opportunities.
Twin Peaks / Glen Park; steep lots often require site visits for accurate estimates.
Sample non-served ZIP used to illustrate out-of-coverage responses in the FAQ and support tools.
